test.template: Run pytest with coverage: `uv run pytest --tb=short -v --cov`. Show all failures with tracebacks and suggest fixes. If no pytest config exists, check for pyproject.toml or setup.cfg.

commit.template: Generate a concise commit message for the current changes. Analyze the git diff to understand what was changed and why. Only output the suggested message, do not create the commit.

lint.template: Run the project's linter and fix any issues. If no lint command exists, analyze the code for common issues.

explain.template: Explain the code in $ARGUMENTS. Break down how it works, what it does, and identify any potential issues or improvements.

pr.template: Generate a GitLab merge request title and description for the current branch changes. Run `git log --oneline main..HEAD` and `git diff main..HEAD --stat` to understand the scope. Output only the MR title and a markdown description with a Summary section and bullet points of key changes. Do not create the MR.

venv.template: Set up a Python virtual environment for this project. Use uv if available, otherwise fall back to python -m venv. Install dependencies from requirements.txt, pyproject.toml, or setup.py if present. If $ARGUMENTS is provided, use it as the venv path.

deps.template: Analyze Python dependencies for this project. Check for outdated packages, security vulnerabilities, dependency conflicts, and unused imports. Use pip, uv, or pipdeptree as available. Report findings and suggest fixes.

reviewer.prompt: You are a code reviewer. Focus on security, performance, maintainability, and best practices. Provide actionable feedback and suggest improvements.

refactor.prompt: You are a refactoring specialist. Focus on improving code quality, reducing complexity, and making the code more maintainable while preserving functionality.

docs.prompt: You are a technical documentation writer. Create clear, concise documentation that explains the code's purpose, usage, and any important considerations.

instructions: For Python projects, prefer ruff for linting and formatting. Use type hints consistently. Follow PEP 8 conventions.
When running or suggesting test commands, use pytest with uv run (e.g. `uv run pytest`). Prefer pytest fixtures over unittest setUp/tearDown. Use `--tb=short` for readable tracebacks.
When working with containers, use podman instead of docker. Commands are drop-in compatible (e.g. `podman ps`, `podman build`, `podman-compose`).
When looking up library documentation, use the webfetch tool with the official docs URL (e.g. docs.python.org, readthedocs.io, pypi.org).
